text.NRSV: Long ago God spoke to our ancestors in many and various ways by the prophets,
text.Rotherham: Whereas, in many parts and in many ways of old, God spake unto the fathers, in the prophets,
text.DRB: Diversely and many ways in times past God speaking to the fathers in the prophets,
text.KJV: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,
text.NABRE: In times past, God spoke in partial and various ways to our ancestors through the prophets;

text.NRSV: but in these last days he has spoken to us by a Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom he also created the worlds.
text.Rotherham: At the end of these days, He hath spoken unto us in his Son,—whom he hath appointed heir of all things, through whom also he hath made the ages;
text.DRB: last of all in these days hath spoken to us in his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all, by whom he made also the worlds.
text.KJV: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;
text.NABRE: in these last days, he spoke to us through a son, whom he made heir of all things and through whom he created the universe,

text.NRSV: He is the reflection of God’s glory and the exact imprint of God’s very being, and he sustains all things by his powerful word. When he had made purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
text.Rotherham: Who, being an eradiated brightness of his glory, and an exact representation of his very being, also bearing up all things by the utterance of his power, purification of sins, having achieved, sat down on the right hand of the majesty in high places:
text.DRB: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the figure of his substance, and carrying all things by the word of his power, making purgation of sins, sitteth on the right hand of the Majesty in the high places:
text.KJV: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
text.NABRE: who is the refulgence of his glory, the very imprint of his being, and who sustains all things by his mighty word. When he had accomplished purification from sins, he took his seat at the right hand of the Majesty on high,

text.NRSV: having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
text.Rotherham: By so much becoming superior to the messengers, by as much as, going beyond them, he hath inherited a more distinguished name.
text.DRB: being made so much better than Angels, as he hath inherited a more excellent name above them.
text.KJV: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
text.NABRE: as far superior to the angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs. II. The Son Higher Than the Angels Messianic Enthronement.

text.NRSV: For to which of the angels did God ever say, “You are my Son; today I have begotten you”? Or again, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son”?
text.Rotherham: For unto which of the messengers said he at any time—My Son, art, thou, I, this day have begotten thee? and again—I, will become, his father, and, he, shall become my Son?
text.DRB: For to which of the Angels hath he said at any time, Thou art my Son, today have I begotten thee? and again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son.
text.KJV: For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son?
text.NABRE: For to which of the angels did God ever say: “You are my son; this day I have begotten you”? Or again: “I will be a father to him, and he shall be a son to me”?

text.NRSV: And again, when he brings the firstborn into the world, he says, “Let all God’s angels worship him.”
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever he again introduceth the first-begotten into the habitable earth, he saith—And let all God’s messengers worship him!
text.DRB: And when again he bringeth in the first-begotten into the world, he saith, And let all the Angels of God adore him.
text.KJV: And again, when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world, he saith, And let all the angels of God worship him.
text.NABRE: And again, when he leads the first-born into the world, he says: “Let all the angels of God worship him.”

text.NRSV: Of the angels he says, “He makes his angels winds, and his servants flames of fire.”
text.Rotherham: Even as to the messengers, indeed, he saith—Who maketh his messengers, winds, and his ministers of state, a fiery flame;
text.DRB: And to the Angels truly he saith, He that maketh his Angels, spirits: and his ministers, a flame of fire.
text.KJV: And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.
text.NABRE: Of the angels he says: “He makes his angels winds and his ministers a fiery flame”;

text.NRSV: But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, and the righteous scepter is the scepter of your kingdom.
text.Rotherham: but, as to the Son,—Thy throne, O God, is unto times age-abiding, and—A sceptre of equity, is the sceptre of his kingdom,
text.DRB: But to the Son: Thy throne o God for ever and ever: a rod of equity, the rod of thy kingdom.
text.KJV: But unto the Son he saith, Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom.
text.NABRE: but of the Son: “Your throne, O God, stands forever and ever; and a righteous scepter is the scepter of your kingdom.

text.NRSV: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions.”
text.Rotherham: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated lawlessness,—For this cause, hath God, thy God, anointed thee with the oil of exultation, beyond thy partners;
text.DRB: Thou hast loved justice, and hated iniquity: therefore thee, God, thy God hath anointed with the oil of exultation above thy fellows.
text.KJV: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
text.NABRE: You loved justice and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, anointed you with the oil of gladness above your companions”;

text.NRSV: And, “In the beginning, Lord, you founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands;
text.Rotherham: and—Thou, by way of beginning, Lord, the earth, didst found, and, the works of thy hands, are the heavens,—
text.DRB: And, Thou in the beginning o Lord didst found the earth: and the works of thy hands are the heavens.
text.KJV: And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine hands:
text.NABRE: and: “At the beginning, O Lord, you established the earth, and the heavens are the works of your hands.

text.NRSV: they will perish, but you remain; they will all wear out like clothing;
text.Rotherham: They, shall perish, but, thou, abidest still, and, all, as a mantle, shall be worn out,
text.DRB: They shall perish, but thou shalt continue: and they shall all wax old as a garment.
text.KJV: They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;
text.NABRE: They will perish, but you remain; and they will all grow old like a garment.

text.NRSV: like a cloak you will roll them up, and like clothing they will be changed. But you are the same, and your years will never end.”
text.Rotherham: And, as if a robe, wilt thou fold them up,—as a mantle, and they shall be changed; but, thou, art the same, and thy years shall not fail.
text.DRB: And as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed: but thou art the self-same, and thy years shall not fail.
text.KJV: And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.
text.NABRE: You will roll them up like a cloak, and like a garment they will be changed. But you are the same, and your years will have no end.”

text.NRSV: But to which of the angels has he ever said, “Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet”?
text.Rotherham: But, to which of the messengers, hath he said, at any time—Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thy foes thy footstool?
text.DRB: But to which of the Angels said he at any time: Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies the foot-stool of thy feet?
text.KJV: But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?
text.NABRE: But to which of the angels has he ever said: “Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool”?

text.NRSV: Are not all angels spirits in the divine service, sent to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?
text.Rotherham: Are they not, all, spirits, doing public service,—for ministry, sent forth, for the sake of them who are about to inherit salvation?
text.DRB: Are they not all, ministering spirits: sent to minister for them which shall receive the inheritance of salvation?
text.KJV: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?
text.NABRE: Are they not all ministering spirits sent to serve, for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?

text.NRSV: Therefore we must pay greater attention to what we have heard, so that we do not drift away from it.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, it behoveth us, with unwonted firmness, to be holding fast unto the things that have been heard, lest, at any time, we drift away.
text.DRB: Therefore more aboundantly ought we to observe those things which we have heard: lest perhaps we run out.
text.KJV: Therefore we ought to give the more earnest heed to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let them slip.
text.NABRE: Therefore, we must attend all the more to what we have heard, so that we may not be carried away.

text.NRSV: For if the message declared through angels was valid, and every transgression or disobedience received a just penalty,
text.Rotherham: For, if the word through messengers spoken became firm, and, every transgression and disobedience received a just recompense,
text.DRB: For if the word that was spoken by Angels, became sure, and all prevarication and disobedience hath received a just retribution of reward:
text.KJV: For if the word spoken by angels was stedfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompence of reward;
text.NABRE: For if the word announced through angels proved firm, and every transgression and disobedience received its just recompense,

text.NRSV: how can we escape if we neglect so great a salvation? It was declared at first through the Lord, and it was attested to us by those who heard him,
text.Rotherham: how shall, we, escape, if, so great a salvation as this, we have neglected,—which, indeed, having received, a beginning, of being spoken through the Lord, by them who heard, unto us was confirmed,
text.DRB: how shall we escape if we neglect so great salvation? which when it was begun to be declared by our Lord, of them that heard was confirmed on us,
text.KJV: How shall we escape, if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard him;
text.NABRE: how shall we escape if we ignore so great a salvation? Announced originally through the Lord, it was confirmed for us by those who had heard.

text.NRSV: while God added his testimony by signs and wonders and various miracles, and by gifts of the Holy Spirit, distributed according to his will.
text.Rotherham: God, jointly witnessing also, both with signs and wonders and manifold mighty works, and with distributions of Holy Spirit, according to his own will?
text.DRB: God withal testifying by signs, and wonders, and diverse miracles, and distributions of the Holy Ghost according to his will.
text.KJV: God also bearing them witness, both with signs and wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own will?
text.NABRE: God added his testimony by signs, wonders, various acts of power, and distribution of the gifts of the holy Spirit according to his will. Exaltation Through Abasement.

text.NRSV: Now God did not subject the coming world, about which we are speaking, to angels.
text.Rotherham: For, not unto messengers, hath he subjected the coming habitable earth of which we are speaking;
text.DRB: For not to Angels hath God made subject the world to come, whereof we speak.
text.KJV: For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.
text.NABRE: For it was not to angels that he subjected the world to come, of which we are speaking.

text.NRSV: But someone has testified somewhere, “What are human beings that you are mindful of them, or mortals, that you care for them?
text.Rotherham: But one somewhere hath borne witness, saying—What is man, that thou shouldst make mention of him? Or the son of man, that thou shouldst put him in charge?
text.DRB: But one hath testified in a certain place, saying: What is man, that thou art mindful of him: or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
text.KJV: But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
text.NABRE: Instead, someone has testified somewhere: “What is man that you are mindful of him, or the son of man that you care for him?

text.NRSV: You have made them for a little while lower than the angels; you have crowned them with glory and honor,
text.Rotherham: Thou hast made him less, some little, than messengers, With glory and honour, hast thou crowned him,— [And hast set him over the works of thy hands];
text.DRB: Thou didst minish him little less than Angels: with glory and honour thou hast crowned him, and constituted him over the works of thy hands.
text.KJV: Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:
text.NABRE: You made him for a little while lower than the angels; you crowned him with glory and honor,

text.NRSV: subjecting all things under their feet.” Now in subjecting all things to them, God left nothing outside their control. As it is, we do not yet see everything in subjection to them,
text.Rotherham: All things, hast thou subjected beneath his feet. For, in subjecting, [to him] the all things, nothing, left he, to him unsubjected; But now, not yet, do we see, to him, the all things subjected;—
text.DRB: All things hast thou made subject under his feet. For in that he subjected all things to him, he left nothing not subject to him. But now we see not as yet all things subjected to him.
text.KJV: Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing that is not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.
text.NABRE: subjecting all things under his feet.” In “subjecting” all things [to him], he left nothing not “subject to him.” Yet at present we do not see “all things subject to him,”

text.NRSV: but we do see Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels, now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death, so that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, made some little less than messengers, we do behold: by reason of the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour, to the end that, by favour of God, in behalf of every one, he might taste of death.
text.DRB: But him that was a little lessened under the Angels, we see Jesus, because of the passion of death, crowned with glory and honour: that through the grace of God he might taste death for all.
text.KJV: But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.
text.NABRE: but we do see Jesus “crowned with glory and honor” because he suffered death, he who “for a little while” was made “lower than the angels,” that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone.

text.NRSV: It was fitting that God, for whom and through whom all things exist, in bringing many children to glory, should make the pioneer of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
text.Rotherham: For it was becoming in him—For the sake of whom are the all things, and by means of whom are the all things,—when, many sons, unto glory, he would lead, The Princely Leader of their salvation, through sufferings, to make perfect.
text.DRB: For it became him for whom all things, and by whom all things, that had brought many children into glory, to consummate the author of their salvation, by his passion.
text.KJV: For it became him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
text.NABRE: For it was fitting that he, for whom and through whom all things exist, in bringing many children to glory, should make the leader to their salvation perfect through suffering.

text.NRSV: For the one who sanctifies and those who are sanctified all have one Father. For this reason Jesus is not ashamed to call them brothers and sisters,
text.Rotherham: For, both he that maketh holy, and they who are being made holy, are, all, of One; For which cause, he is not ashamed to be calling them, brethren,
text.DRB: For he that sanctifieth, and they that be sanctified; all of one. For the which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren,
text.KJV: For both he that sanctifieth and they who are sanctified are all of one: for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren,
text.NABRE: He who consecrates and those who are being consecrated all have one origin. Therefore, he is not ashamed to call them “brothers,”

text.NRSV: saying, “I will proclaim your name to my brothers and sisters, in the midst of the congregation I will praise you.”
text.Rotherham: saying—I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of an assembly, will I sing praise unto thee;
text.DRB: saying, I will declare thy name to my brethren: in the midst of the Church will I praise thee.
text.KJV: Saying, I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.
text.NABRE: saying: “I will proclaim your name to my brothers, in the midst of the assembly I will praise you”;

text.NRSV: And again, “I will put my trust in him.” And again, “Here am I and the children whom God has given me.”
text.Rotherham: and again—I, will be confident upon him; and again—Lo! I, and the children which, unto me, God, hath given.
text.DRB: And again, I will have affiance in him. And again, Behold here am I & my children: whom God hath given me.
text.KJV: And again, I will put my trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.
text.NABRE: and again: “I will put my trust in him”; and again: “Behold, I and the children God has given me.”

text.NRSV: Since, therefore, the children share flesh and blood, he himself likewise shared the same things, so that through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death, that is, the devil,
text.Rotherham: Seeing therefore the children have received a fellowship of blood and flesh, he also, in like manner, took partnership in the same,—in order that, through death,—he might paralyse him that held the dominion of death, that is, the Adversary,—
text.DRB: Therefore because the children have communicated with flesh and blood, himself also in like manner hath been partaker of the same: that by death he might destroy him that had the empire of death, that is to say, the devil:
text.KJV: Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of flesh and blood, he also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the devil;
text.NABRE: Now since the children share in blood and flesh, he likewise shared in them, that through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death, that is, the devil,

text.NRSV: and free those who all their lives were held in slavery by the fear of death.
text.Rotherham: And might release these—as many as, by fear of death, were all their lifetime liable, to bondage.
text.DRB: and might deliver them that by the fear of death through all their life were subject to servitude.
text.KJV: And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.
text.NABRE: and free those who through fear of death had been subject to slavery all their life.

text.NRSV: For it is clear that he did not come to help angels, but the descendants of Abraham.
text.Rotherham: For, not surely of messengers, is he laying hold, but, of Abraham’s seed, he is laying hold.
text.DRB: For no where doth he take Angels: but the seed of Abraham he taketh.
text.KJV: For verily he took not on him the nature of angels; but he took on him the seed of Abraham.
text.NABRE: Surely he did not help angels but rather the descendants of Abraham;

text.NRSV: Therefore he had to become like his brothers and sisters in every respect, so that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in the service of God, to make a sacrifice of atonement for the sins of the people.
text.Rotherham: Whence he was obliged, in every way, unto the brethren, to be made like, that he might become a merciful and faithful high-priest, in the things pertaining unto God,—for the making of propitiation for the sins of the people.
text.DRB: Whereupon he ought in all things to be like unto his brethren: that he might become a merciful and faithful high Priest before God, that he might repropitiate the sins of the people.
text.KJV: Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.
text.NABRE: therefore, he had to become like his brothers in every way, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest before God to expiate the sins of the people.

text.NRSV: Because he himself was tested by what he suffered, he is able to help those who are being tested.
text.Rotherham: For, in that, he, suffered when tested, he is able, unto them who are being tested, to give succour.
text.DRB: For in that wherein himself suffered and was tempted: he is able to help them also that are tempted.
text.KJV: For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.
text.NABRE: Because he himself was tested through what he suffered, he is able to help those who are being tested.

text.NRSV: Therefore, brothers and sisters, holy partners in a heavenly calling, consider that Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession,
text.Rotherham: Whence, holy brethren, partners, in a heavenly calling, attentively consider the Apostle and High—priest of our confession—Jesus
text.DRB: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly vocation, consider the Apostle, and high Priest of our confession Jesus:
text.KJV: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus;
text.NABRE: Therefore, holy “brothers,” sharing in a heavenly calling, reflect on Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession,

text.NRSV: was faithful to the one who appointed him, just as Moses also “was faithful in all God’s house.”
text.Rotherham: As one, faithful, to him who made him: as, Moses also, in [all] his house.
text.DRB: who is faithful to him that made him, as also Moyses in all his house.
text.KJV: Who was faithful to him that appointed him, as also Moses was faithful in all his house.
text.NABRE: who was faithful to the one who appointed him, just as Moses was “faithful in [all] his house.”

text.NRSV: Yet Jesus is worthy of more glory than Moses, just as the builder of a house has more honor than the house itself.
text.Rotherham: For, of more glory than Moses, hath, this one been counted worthy—by as much as more honour than, the house, hath, he that prepared it;
text.DRB: For, this man is esteemed worthy of more ample glory above Moyses, by so much as more ample glory than the house, hath he that framed it.
text.KJV: For this man was counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who hath builded the house hath more honour than the house.
text.NABRE: But he is worthy of more “glory” than Moses, as the founder of a house has more “honor” than the house itself.

text.NRSV: (For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.)
text.Rotherham: For, every house, is prepared by someone,—but, he that hath prepared all things, is, God.
text.DRB: For every house is framed of some man. But he that created all things, is God.
text.KJV: For every house is builded by some man; but he that built all things is God.
text.NABRE: Every house is founded by someone, but the founder of all is God.

text.NRSV: Now Moses was faithful in all God’s house as a servant, to testify to the things that would be spoken later.
text.Rotherham: Even Moses, indeed, was faithful in all his house, as an attendant, for a witness of the things which were to be spoken;
text.DRB: And Moyses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be said:
text.KJV: And Moses verily was faithful in all his house, as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;
text.NABRE: Moses was “faithful in all his house” as a “servant” to testify to what would be spoken,

text.NRSV: Christ, however, was faithful over God’s house as a son, and we are his house if we hold firm the confidence and the pride that belong to hope.
text.Rotherham: But, Christ, as, a Son, over his house,—whose house are, we,—if, the freedom of speech and boast of the hope, [throughout, firm,] we hold fast.
text.DRB: but Christ as the Son in his own house: which house are we, if we keep firm the confidence and glory of hope unto the end.
text.KJV: But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.
text.NABRE: but Christ was faithful as a son placed over his house. We are his house, if [only] we hold fast to our confidence and pride in our hope.

text.NRSV: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, “Today, if you hear his voice,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore,—according as saith the Holy Spirit—To-day, if, unto his voice ye would hearken, do not
text.DRB: Wherefore, as the Holy Ghost saith, Today if you shall hear his voice,
text.KJV: Wherefore (as the Holy Ghost saith, To day if ye will hear his voice,
text.NABRE: Israel’s Infidelity a Warning. Therefore, as the holy Spirit says: “Oh, that today you would hear his voice,

text.NRSV: do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, as on the day of testing in the wilderness,
text.Rotherham: harden your hearts,—as in the embitterment, in the day of testing in the desert,
text.DRB: harden not your hearts as in the exacerbation according to the day of tentation in the desert,
text.KJV: Harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness:
text.NABRE: ‘Harden not your hearts as at the rebellion in the day of testing in the desert,

text.NRSV: where your ancestors put me to the test, though they had seen my works
text.Rotherham: When your fathers tested by proving, and saw my works forty years.
text.DRB: where your fathers tempted me: proved and saw my works
text.KJV: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works forty years.
text.NABRE: where your ancestors tested and tried me and saw my works

text.NRSV: for forty years. Therefore I was angry with that generation, and I said, ‘They always go astray in their hearts, and they have not known my ways.’
text.Rotherham: Wherefore I was sore vexed with this generation, and said, Always err they in their heart;—howbeit, they, learned not my ways:
text.DRB: forty years. For the which cause I was offended with this Generation, and said, They do always err in heart. And they have not known my ways.
text.KJV: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in their heart; and they have not known my ways.
text.NABRE: for forty years. Because of this I was provoked with that generation and I said, “They have always been of erring heart, and they do not know my ways.”

text.NRSV: As in my anger I swore, ‘They will not enter my rest.’”
text.Rotherham: So I sware in mine anger—they shall not enter into my rest!—
text.DRB: to whom I sware in my wrath, If they shall enter into my rest.
text.KJV: So I sware in my wrath, They shall not enter into my rest.)
text.NABRE: As I swore in my wrath, “They shall not enter into my rest.”’”

text.NRSV: Take care, brothers and sisters, that none of you may have an evil, unbelieving heart that turns away from the living God.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed, brethren, lest at any time, there shall be in any one of you, a wicked heart of unbelief, in revolting from a Living God.
text.DRB: Beware brethren, lest perhaps there be in some of you an evil heart of incredulity, to depart from the living God.
text.KJV: Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God.
text.NABRE: Take care, brothers, that none of you may have an evil and unfaithful heart, so as to forsake the living God.

text.NRSV: But exhort one another every day, as long as it is called “today,” so that none of you may be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
text.Rotherham: But be exhorting one another, on each successive day,—while the To-day is being named! lest any from among you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
text.DRB: But exhort yourselves every day, whiles today is named, that none of you be obdurate with the fallacy of sin.
text.KJV: But exhort one another daily, while it is called To day; lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.
text.NABRE: Encourage yourselves daily while it is still “today,” so that none of you may grow hardened by the deceit of sin.

text.NRSV: For we have become partners of Christ, if only we hold our first confidence firm to the end.
text.Rotherham: For, partners of the Christ, have we become,—if, at least, the beginning of the confidence, throughout, firm, we hold fast:
text.DRB: For we be made partakers of Christ: yet so if we keep the beginning of his substance firm unto the end.
text.KJV: For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence stedfast unto the end;
text.NABRE: We have become partners of Christ if only we hold the beginning of the reality firm until the end,

text.NRSV: As it is said, “Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.”
text.Rotherham: So long as it is said—To-day, if, unto his voice, ye would hearken, do not harden your hearts,—as in the embitterment.
text.DRB: While it is said, Today if you shall hear his voice, do not obdurate your hearts as in that exacerbation.
text.KJV: While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation.
text.NABRE: for it is said: “Oh, that today you would hear his voice: ‘Harden not your hearts as at the rebellion.’”

text.NRSV: Now who were they who heard and yet were rebellious? Was it not all those who left Egypt under the leadership of Moses?
text.Rotherham: For, who, though they heard, caused embitterment? Nay, indeed! did not all who come forth out of Egypt through Moses?
text.DRB: For some hearing did exasperate: but not all they that went out of Ægypt by Moyses.
text.KJV: For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.
text.NABRE: Who were those who rebelled when they heard? Was it not all those who came out of Egypt under Moses?

text.NRSV: But with whom was he angry forty years? Was it not those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
text.Rotherham: But, with whom, was be sore vexed forty years? Was it not with them who sinned, whose dead bodies fell in the desert?
text.DRB: And with whom was he offended forty years? was it not with them that sinned, whose carcasses were overthrown in the desert?
text.KJV: But with whom was he grieved forty years? was it not with them that had sinned, whose carcases fell in the wilderness?
text.NABRE: With whom was he “provoked for forty years”? Was it not those who had sinned, whose corpses fell in the desert?

text.NRSV: And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, if not to those who were disobedient?
text.Rotherham: But, unto whom, sware he, that they should not enter into his rest,—save unto them who were obstinate?
text.DRB: And to whom did he swear that they should not enter into his rest: but to them that were incredulous?
text.KJV: And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?
text.NABRE: And to whom did he “swear that they should not enter into his rest,” if not to those who were disobedient?

text.NRSV: So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.
text.Rotherham: And we see, that they were not able to enter, because of unbelief.
text.DRB: And we see that they could not enter in, because of incredulity.
text.KJV: So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.
text.NABRE: And we see that they could not enter for lack of faith.

text.NRSV: Therefore, while the promise of entering his rest is still open, let us take care that none of you should seem to have failed to reach it.
text.Rotherham: Let us therefore fear, lest at any time, although there is left behind a promise of entering into his rest,—any one from amongst you should be deemed, to have come short;
text.DRB: Let us fear therefore lest perhaps forsaking the promise of entering into his rest, some of you be thought to be wanting.
text.KJV: Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left us of entering into his rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.
text.NABRE: Therefore, let us be on our guard while the promise of entering into his rest remains, that none of you seem to have failed.

text.NRSV: For indeed the good news came to us just as to them; but the message they heard did not benefit them, because they were not united by faith with those who listened.
text.Rotherham: For we have had delivered to us the joyful message, just as even, they; but the word which was heard did not profit them, they not having been blended, by faith, with the things heard.
text.DRB: For to us also it hath been denounced, as also to them. But the word of hearing did not profit them, not mixt with faith of those things which they heard.
text.KJV: For unto us was the gospel preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in them that heard it.
text.NABRE: For in fact we have received the good news just as they did. But the word that they heard did not profit them, for they were not united in faith with those who listened.

text.NRSV: For we who have believed enter that rest, just as God has said, “As in my anger I swore, ‘They shall not enter my rest,’” though his works were finished at the foundation of the world.
text.Rotherham: For we who have believed, are to enter into the rest, according as he hath said—So I sware in mine anger, They shall not enter into my rest; And yet, the works, from the foundation of the world, had been brought into existence,
text.DRB: For we that have believed, shall enter into the rest: as he said, As I sware in my wrath, if they shall enter into my rest: & truly the works from the foundation of the world being perfited.
text.KJV: For we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.
text.NABRE: For we who believed enter into [that] rest, just as he has said: “As I swore in my wrath, ‘They shall not enter into my rest,’” and yet his works were accomplished at the foundation of the world.

text.NRSV: For in one place it speaks about the seventh day as follows, “And God rested on the seventh day from all his works.”
text.Rotherham: For he hath spoken, somewhere, concerning the seventh [day], thus—And God rested, on the seventh day, from all his works;
text.DRB: For he said in a certain place of the seventh day, thus: And God rested the seventh day from all his works.
text.KJV: For he spake in a certain place of the seventh day on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.
text.NABRE: For he has spoken somewhere about the seventh day in this manner, “And God rested on the seventh day from all his works”;

text.NRSV: And again in this place it says, “They shall not enter my rest.”
text.Rotherham: And in this again—They shall not enter into my rest.
text.DRB: And again in this, If they shall enter into my rest.
text.KJV: And in this place again, If they shall enter into my rest.
text.NABRE: and again, in the previously mentioned place, “They shall not enter into my rest.”

text.NRSV: Since therefore it remains open for some to enter it, and those who formerly received the good news failed to enter because of disobedience,
text.Rotherham: Seeing, therefore, that it is left over for, some, to enter into it, and, they who formerly had delivered to them the joyful message, entered not in by reason of obstinacy,—
text.DRB: Because then it remaineth that certain enter into it, and they to whom first it was preached, did not enter because of incredulity:
text.KJV: Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of unbelief:
text.NABRE: Therefore, since it remains that some will enter into it, and those who formerly received the good news did not enter because of disobedience,

text.NRSV: again he sets a certain day—“today”—saying through David much later, in the words already quoted, “Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts.”
text.Rotherham: Again, he marketh out a certain day, To-day, in David, saying—after so long a time as this,—according as it hath been said before:—To-day, if, unto his voice, ye would hearken, do not harden your hearts.
text.DRB: again he limiteth a certain day: Today, in David saying, after so long time, as is above said, Today if you shall hear his voice: do not obdurate your hearts.
text.KJV: Again, he limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
text.NABRE: he once more set a day, “today,” when long afterwards he spoke through David, as already quoted: “Oh, that today you would hear his voice: ‘Harden not your hearts.’”

text.NRSV: For if Joshua had given them rest, God would not speak later about another day.
text.Rotherham: For, if, unto them, Joshua had given rest, it had not in that case, concerning another day, been speaking, after, these things.
text.DRB: For if Jesus had given them rest: he would never speak of another day afterward.
text.KJV: For if Jesus had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.
text.NABRE: Now if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterwards of another day.

text.NRSV: So then, a sabbath rest still remains for the people of God;
text.Rotherham: Hence there is, left over, a Sabbath-keeping, for the people of God.
text.DRB: Therefore there is left a sabbatism for the people of God.
text.KJV: There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.
text.NABRE: Therefore, a sabbath rest still remains for the people of God.

text.NRSV: for those who enter God’s rest also cease from their labors as God did from his.
text.Rotherham: For, he that hath entered into his rest, He too, hath rested from his works, just as, from his own, God, [rested].
text.DRB: For he that is entered into his rest, the same also hath rested from his works, as God from his.
text.KJV: For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.
text.NABRE: And whoever enters into God’s rest, rests from his own works as God did from his.

text.NRSV: Let us therefore make every effort to enter that rest, so that no one may fall through such disobedience as theirs.
text.Rotherham: Let us, therefore, give diligence to enter into that rest, lest anyone fall into the same example, of obstinacy.
text.DRB: Let us hasten therefore to enter into that rest: that no man fall into the same example of incredulity.
text.KJV: Let us labour therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of unbelief.
text.NABRE: Therefore, let us strive to enter into that rest, so that no one may fall after the same example of disobedience.

text.NRSV: Indeed, the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing until it divides soul from spirit, joints from marrow; it is able to judge the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
text.Rotherham: For, living, is the word of God, and, energetic, and more cutting than any knife with two edges, and penetrating as far as a dividing asunder of soul and spirit, of joints also, and marrow, and able to judge the impulses and designs of the heart;
text.DRB: For the word of God is lively and forcible, and more piercing than any two-edged sword: & reaching unto the division of the soul and the spirit, of the joints also and the marrows, and a discerner of the cogitations and intents of the heart.
text.KJV: For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.
text.NABRE: Indeed, the word of God is living and effective, sharper than any two-edged sword, penetrating even between soul and spirit, joints and marrow, and able to discern reflections and thoughts of the heart.

text.NRSV: And before him no creature is hidden, but all are naked and laid bare to the eyes of the one to whom we must render an account.
text.Rotherham: And there is, no created thing, can be secreted before him, but, all things, are naked and exposed to his eyes:—as to whom is, our discourse.
text.DRB: And there is no creature invisible in his sight. But all things are naked and open to his eyes, to whom our speech is.
text.KJV: Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.
text.NABRE: No creature is concealed from him, but everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of him to whom we must render an account.

text.NRSV: Since, then, we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold fast to our confession.
text.Rotherham: Having then a great high-priest who hath passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast the confession;
text.DRB: Having therefore a great high Priest that hath entered the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold the confession.
text.KJV: Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our profession.
text.NABRE: Jesus, Compassionate High Priest. Therefore, since we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold fast to our confession.

text.NRSV: For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but we have one who in every respect has been tested as we are, yet without sin.
text.Rotherham: For we have not a high-priest unable to have fellow-feeling with our weaknesses, but one tested in all respects, by way of likeness, apart from sin.
text.DRB: For we have not a high priest that cannot have compassion on our infirmities: but tempted in all things by similitude, except sin.
text.KJV: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin.
text.NABRE: For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but one who has similarly been tested in every way, yet without sin.

text.NRSV: Let us therefore approach the throne of grace with boldness, so that we may receive mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
text.Rotherham: Let us, then, be approaching with freedom of speech, unto the throne of favour, that we may receive mercy, and, favour, may find, for seasonable succour.
text.DRB: Let us go therefore with confidence to the throne of grace: that we may obtain mercy, and find grace in seasonable aid.
text.KJV: Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.
text.NABRE: So let us confidently approach the throne of grace to receive mercy and to find grace for timely help.

text.NRSV: Every high priest chosen from among mortals is put in charge of things pertaining to God on their behalf, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
text.Rotherham: For, every high-priest who from among men is taken, on behalf of men, is appointed, as to the things pertaining unto God, that he may be offering [both] gifts and sacrifices for sins,—
text.KJV: For every high priest taken from among men is ordained for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins:
text.NABRE: Every high priest is taken from among men and made their representative before God, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
text.DRB: For every high Priest taken from among men, is appointed for men in those things that pertain to God: that he may offer gifts and sacrifices for sins:

text.NRSV: He is able to deal gently with the ignorant and wayward, since he himself is subject to weakness;
text.Rotherham: Able, to have a measure of feeling, for the ignorant and erring,—since, he also, is compassed with weakness;
text.KJV: Who can have compassion on the ignorant, and on them that are out of the way; for that he himself also is compassed with infirmity.
text.NABRE: He is able to deal patiently with the ignorant and erring, for he himself is beset by weakness
text.DRB: that can have compassion on them that be ignorant and do err: because himself also is compassed with infirmity:

text.NRSV: and because of this he must offer sacrifice for his own sins as well as for those of the people.
text.Rotherham: And, for this cause, is he obliged—as for the people, so also, for himself,—to be offering for sins;
text.KJV: And by reason hereof he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.
text.NABRE: and so, for this reason, must make sin offerings for himself as well as for the people.
text.DRB: & therefore he ought, as for the people, so also for himself to offer for sins.

text.NRSV: And one does not presume to take this honor, but takes it only when called by God, just as Aaron was.
text.Rotherham: And, not unto himself, doth one take the honour, but when called by God, just as, even Aaron:
text.KJV: And no man taketh this honour unto himself, but he that is called of God, as was Aaron.
text.NABRE: No one takes this honor upon himself but only when called by God, just as Aaron was.
text.DRB: Neither doth any man take the honour to himself, but he that is called of God, as Aaron.

text.NRSV: So also Christ did not glorify himself in becoming a high priest, but was appointed by the one who said to him, “You are my Son, today I have begotten you”;
text.Rotherham: Thus, also the Christ, glorified not himself to become a high-priest, but he that spake unto him—My Son, art, thou, I, this day, have begotten thee;
text.KJV: So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him, Thou art my Son, to day have I begotten thee.
text.NABRE: In the same way, it was not Christ who glorified himself in becoming high priest, but rather the one who said to him: “You are my son; this day I have begotten you”;
text.DRB: So Christ also did not glorify himself that he might be made a high priest; but he that spake to him, My Son art thou, I this day have begotten thee.

text.NRSV: as he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.”
text.Rotherham: As also, in a different place, he saith—Thou, art a priest, age-abidingly, according to the rank of Melchizedek:
text.KJV: As he saith also in another place, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
text.NABRE: just as he says in another place: “You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.”
text.DRB: As also in another place he saith, Thou art a priest for ever, according to the order of Melchisedech.

text.NRSV: In the days of his flesh, Jesus offered up prayers and supplications, with loud cries and tears, to the one who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverent submission.
text.Rotherham: Who, in the days of his flesh, having offered up, both supplications and entreaties unto him that was able to save him out of death, with mighty outcries and tears, and been hearkened to by reason of his devoutness,
text.KJV: Who in the days of his flesh, when he had offered up prayers and supplications with strong crying and tears unto him that was able to save him from death, and was heard in that he feared;
text.NABRE: In the days when he was in the flesh, he offered prayers and supplications with loud cries and tears to the one who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverence.
text.DRB: Who in the days of his flesh, with a strong cry and tears, offering prayers and supplications to him that could save him from death, was heard for his reverence.

text.NRSV: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience through what he suffered;
text.Rotherham: Even though he was a son, yet learned, from what things he suffered, obedience;
text.KJV: Though he were a Son, yet learned he obedience by the things which he suffered;
text.NABRE: Son though he was, he learned obedience from what he suffered;
text.DRB: And truly whereas he was the Son, he learned by those things which he suffered, obedience:

text.NRSV: and having been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him,
text.Rotherham: And, being made perfect, became, to all them that obey him, Author of salvation age-abiding;
text.KJV: And being made perfect, he became the author of eternal salvation unto all them that obey him;
text.NABRE: and when he was made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him,
text.DRB: and being consummate, was made to all that obey him, cause of eternal salvation,

text.NRSV: having been designated by God a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
text.Rotherham: Being addressed by God as high-priest—according to the rank of Melchizedek.
text.KJV: Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.
text.NABRE: declared by God high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
text.DRB: called of God a high priest according to the order of Melchisedech.

text.NRSV: About this we have much to say that is hard to explain, since you have become dull in understanding.
text.Rotherham: Concerning whom, great, is our discourse, and of difficult interpretation, to express, seeing that, slothful, have ye become in the hearing;
text.KJV: Of whom we have many things to say, and hard to be uttered, seeing ye are dull of hearing.
text.NABRE: About this we have much to say, and it is difficult to explain, for you have become sluggish in hearing.
text.DRB: Of whom we have great speech and inexplicable to utter: because you are become weak to hear.

text.NRSV: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the basic elements of the oracles of God. You need milk, not solid food;
text.Rotherham: For, even when ye ought to be teachers, by reason of the time, again, have ye, need, that one be teaching, you, what are the first principles of the oracles of God, and have become such as have, need, of milk, not, of strong food;
text.KJV: For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which be the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of milk, and not of strong meat.
text.NABRE: Although you should be teachers by this time, you need to have someone teach you again the basic elements of the utterances of God. You need milk, [and] not solid food.
text.DRB: For whereas you ought to be masters for your time, you need to be taught again yourselves what be the elements of the beginning of the words of God: & you are become such as have need of milk, and not of strong meat.

text.NRSV: for everyone who lives on milk, being still an infant, is unskilled in the word of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: For, every one partaking of milk, is unskilled in discourse of righteousness, for he is, a babe;
text.KJV: For every one that useth milk is unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.
text.NABRE: Everyone who lives on milk lacks experience of the word of righteousness, for he is a child.
text.DRB: For every one that is partaker of milk, is unskillful of the word of justice: for he is a child.

text.NRSV: But solid food is for the mature, for those whose faculties have been trained by practice to distinguish good from evil.
text.Rotherham: But, to such as are mature, pertaineth, the strong food, to them who, by reason of habit, have their organs of perception well trained for discriminating both good and evil.
text.KJV: But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age, even those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.
text.NABRE: But solid food is for the mature, for those whose faculties are trained by practice to discern good and evil.
text.DRB: But strong meat is for the perfect, them that by custom have their senses exercised to the discerning of good and evil.

text.NRSV: Therefore let us go on toward perfection, leaving behind the basic teaching about Christ, and not laying again the foundation: repentance from dead works and faith toward God,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, dismissing the elementary discourse concerning the Christ, unto maturity, let us be tending, not, again, a foundation, laying down—of repentance from dead works, and of faith towards God,
text.DRB: Wherefore intermitting the word of the beginning of Christ, let us proceed to perfection, not again laying the foundation of penance from dead works, & of faith toward God,
text.KJV: Therefore leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto perfection; not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God,
text.NABRE: Therefore, let us leave behind the basic teaching about Christ and advance to maturity, without laying the foundation all over again: repentance from dead works and faith in God,

text.NRSV: instruction about baptisms, laying on of hands, resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
text.Rotherham: Of immersions—in respect of teaching, and of the laying on of hands, of the resurrection of the dead, and of judgment age-abiding;—
text.DRB: of the doctrine of baptisms, & of imposition of hands, & of the resurrection of the dead, & of eternal judgement.
text.KJV: Of the doctrine of baptisms, and of laying on of hands, and of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.
text.NABRE: instruction about baptisms and laying on of hands, resurrection of the dead and eternal judgment.

text.NRSV: And we will do this, if God permits.
text.Rotherham: And, this, will we do, if at least, God, permit.
text.DRB: And this shall we do, if God will permit.
text.KJV: And this will we do, if God permit.
text.NABRE: And we shall do this, if only God permits.

text.NRSV: For it is impossible to restore again to repentance those who have once been enlightened, and have tasted the heavenly gift, and have shared in the Holy Spirit,
text.Rotherham: For it is impossible—as to these who have been, once for all, illuminated, who have tasted also of the heavenly free-gift, and have been made, partners, in a Holy Spirit,
text.DRB: For it is impossible for them that were once illuminated, have tasted also the heavenly gift, & were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,
text.KJV: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,
text.NABRE: For it is impossible in the case of those who have once been enlightened and tasted the heavenly gift and shared in the holy Spirit

text.NRSV: and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come,
text.Rotherham: And have tasted God’s utterance to be, sweet, mighty works also of a coming age,
text.DRB: have more-over tasted the good word of God, & the powers of the world to come,
text.KJV: And have tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the world to come,
text.NABRE: and tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come,

text.NRSV: and then have fallen away, since on their own they are crucifying again the Son of God and are holding him up to contempt.
text.Rotherham: And who have fallen away, again, to be remoulding them into repentance; seeing they are again crucifying unto themselves the Son of God, and holding him up as an example.
text.DRB: and are fallen, to be renewed again to penance, crucifying again to themselves the Son of God, and making him a mockery.
text.KJV: If they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open shame.
text.NABRE: and then have fallen away, to bring them to repentance again, since they are recrucifying the Son of God for themselves and holding him up to contempt.

text.NRSV: Ground that drinks up the rain falling on it repeatedly, and that produces a crop useful to those for whom it is cultivated, receives a blessing from God.
text.Rotherham: For, land, which hath drunk in, the rain, thereupon ofttimes coming, and which bringeth forth vegetation fit for them for whom it is even cultivated, partaketh of a blessing from God;
text.DRB: For the earth drinking the rain often coming upon it, & bringing forth grass commodious for them by whom it is tilled, receiveth blessing of God.
text.KJV: For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs meet for them by whom it is dressed, receiveth blessing from God:
text.NABRE: Ground that has absorbed the rain falling upon it repeatedly and brings forth crops useful to those for whom it is cultivated receives a blessing from God.

text.NRSV: But if it produces thorns and thistles, it is worthless and on the verge of being cursed; its end is to be burned over.
text.Rotherham: But, should it be bringing forth thorns and briars, it is disapproved, and, unto cursing, nigh,—whose end is for burning.
text.DRB: But bringing forth thorns and briers, it is reprobate, and very near a curse, whose end is, to be burnt.
text.KJV: But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected, and is nigh unto cursing; whose end is to be burned.
text.NABRE: But if it produces thorns and thistles, it is rejected; it will soon be cursed and finally burned.

text.NRSV: Even though we speak in this way, beloved, we are confident of better things in your case, things that belong to salvation.
text.Rotherham: But we are persuaded, concerning you, beloved, the things which are better and which contain salvation, though, even thus we speak.
text.DRB: But we confidently trust of you, my best beloved, better things and nearer to salvation; although we speak thus.
text.KJV: But, beloved, we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.
text.NABRE: But we are sure in your regard, beloved, of better things related to salvation, even though we speak in this way.

text.NRSV: For God is not unjust; he will not overlook your work and the love that you showed for his sake in serving the saints, as you still do.
text.Rotherham: For, not unrighteous, is God, to be forgetful of your work and of the love which ye have shewn forth for his name, in that ye have ministered unto the saints, and are ministering,
text.DRB: For God is not unjust, that he should forget your work & love which you have shewed in his name, which have ministered to the saints and do minister.
text.KJV: For God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.
text.NABRE: For God is not unjust so as to overlook your work and the love you have demonstrated for his name by having served and continuing to serve the holy ones.

text.NRSV: And we want each one of you to show the same diligence so as to realize the full assurance of hope to the very end,
text.Rotherham: But we covet that, each one of you, be shewing forth the same diligence, unto the full assurance of the hope throughout:
text.DRB: And our desire is that every one of you shew forth the same carefulness to the accomplishing of hope unto the end:
text.KJV: And we desire that every one of you do shew the same diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end:
text.NABRE: We earnestly desire each of you to demonstrate the same eagerness for the fulfillment of hope until the end,

text.NRSV: so that you may not become sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
text.Rotherham: In order that, not slothful, ye may become, but imitators of them who, through faith and patience, were becoming heirs of the promises.
text.DRB: that you become not slothful, but imitators of them which by faith and patience shall inherit the promises.
text.KJV: That ye be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
text.NABRE: so that you may not become sluggish, but imitators of those who, through faith and patience, are inheriting the promises.

text.NRSV: When God made a promise to Abraham, because he had no one greater by whom to swear, he swore by himself,
text.Rotherham: For, when to Abraham God made promise, seeing he had no one greater by whom to swear, He sware, by himself,—
text.DRB: For God promising to Abraham, because he had none greater by whom he might swear, he sware by himself,
text.KJV: For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could swear by no greater, he sware by himself,
text.NABRE: God’s Promise Immutable. When God made the promise to Abraham, since he had no one greater by whom to swear, “he swore by himself,”

text.NRSV: saying, “I will surely bless you and multiply you.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Truly, if blessing I will bless thee, and, multiplying, I will multiply thee;
text.DRB: saying, Unless blessing I shall bless thee, and multiplying shall multiply thee.
text.KJV: Saying, Surely blessing I will bless thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee.
text.NABRE: and said, “I will indeed bless you and multiply” you.

text.NRSV: And thus Abraham, having patiently endured, obtained the promise.
text.Rotherham: And, thus, being patient, he attained unto the promise.
text.DRB: And so patiently enduring he obtained the promise.
text.KJV: And so, after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
text.NABRE: And so, after patient waiting, he obtained the promise.

text.NRSV: Human beings, of course, swear by someone greater than themselves, and an oath given as confirmation puts an end to all dispute.
text.Rotherham: For, men, by the greater one, swear, and, with them, an end of all gainsaying by way of confirmation is, the oath:
text.DRB: For men swear by a greater than themselves: and the end of all their controversy, for the confirmation, is an oath.
text.KJV: For men verily swear by the greater: and an oath for confirmation is to them an end of all strife.
text.NABRE: Human beings swear by someone greater than themselves; for them an oath serves as a guarantee and puts an end to all argument.

text.NRSV: In the same way, when God desired to show even more clearly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose, he guaranteed it by an oath,
text.Rotherham: Wherein God, being, more abundantly disposed to shew forth unto the heirs of the promise the unchangeableness of his counsel, mediated, with an oath,—
text.DRB: Wherein God meaning more aboundantly to shew to the heirs of the promise the stability of his counsel, he interposed an oath:
text.KJV: Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his counsel, confirmed it by an oath:
text.NABRE: So when God wanted to give the heirs of his promise an even clearer demonstration of the immutability of his purpose, he intervened with an oath,

text.NRSV: so that through two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible that God would prove false, we who have taken refuge might be strongly encouraged to seize the hope set before us.
text.Rotherham: In order that, through means of two unchangeable things, in which it was impossible for God to make himself false, a mighty consolation, we might have, who have fled along to grasp, the fore-lying hope,
text.DRB: that by two things unmoveable, whereby it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a most strong comfort. Who have fled to hold fast the hope proposed,
text.KJV: That by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us:
text.NABRE: so that by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we who have taken refuge might be strongly encouraged to hold fast to the hope that lies before us.

text.NRSV: We have this hope, a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul, a hope that enters the inner shrine behind the curtain,
text.Rotherham: Which we have, as an anchor of the soul, both secure and firm, and entering into the interior of the veil:
text.DRB: which we have as an anchor of the soul, sure and firm, and going in into the inner parts of the veil,
text.KJV: Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and stedfast, and which entereth into that within the veil;
text.NABRE: This we have as an anchor of the soul, sure and firm, which reaches into the interior behind the veil,

text.NRSV: where Jesus, a forerunner on our behalf, has entered, having become a high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.
text.Rotherham: Where a forerunner in our behalf hath entered, even Jesus, who, according to the rank of Melchizedek, hath become, a high-priest unto times age-abiding.
text.DRB: where Jesus the precursor for us is entered, made a high Priest for ever according to the order of Melchisedech.
text.KJV: Whither the forerunner is for us entered, even Jesus, made an high priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
text.NABRE: where Jesus has entered on our behalf as forerunner, becoming high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.

text.NRSV: This “King Melchizedek of Salem, priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he was returning from defeating the kings and blessed him”;
text.Rotherham: For, this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him,—
text.DRB: For this Melchisedech, the king of Salem, Priest of the God most high, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him:
text.KJV: For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;
text.NABRE: This “Melchizedek, king of Salem and priest of God Most High,” “met Abraham as he returned from his defeat of the kings” and “blessed him.”

text.NRSV: and to him Abraham apportioned “one-tenth of everything.” His name, in the first place, means “king of righteousness”; next he is also king of Salem, that is, “king of peace.”
text.Rotherham: To whom even a tenth of all Abraham apportioned, first, indeed, when translated, King of Righteousness, but, after that, King of Salem also, which is King of Peace,—
text.DRB: to whom also Abraham divided tithes of all: first indeed by interpretation, the king of justice: & then also king of Salem, which is to say, king of peace,
text.KJV: To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness, and after that also King of Salem, which is, King of peace;
text.NABRE: And Abraham apportioned to him “a tenth of everything.” His name first means righteous king, and he was also “king of Salem,” that is, king of peace.

text.NRSV: Without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but resembling the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.
text.Rotherham: Without father, without mother, without pedigree, having, neither beginning of days, nor of life an end, but made like unto the Son of God, abideth a priest evermore.
text.DRB: without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but likened to the Son of God, continueth a Priest for ever.
text.KJV: Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.
text.NABRE: Without father, mother, or ancestry, without beginning of days or end of life, thus made to resemble the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.

text.NRSV: See how great he is! Even Abraham the patriarch gave him a tenth of the spoils.
text.Rotherham: Now consider, how great, this man was, to whom, a tenth, Abraham gave out of the choicest spoils, [Yea Abraham] the Patriarch.
text.DRB: And behold how great this man is, to whom also Abraham the Patriarch gave tithes of the principal things.
text.KJV: Now consider how great this man was, unto whom even the patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.
text.NABRE: See how great he is to whom the patriarch “Abraham [indeed] gave a tenth” of his spoils.

text.NRSV: And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to collect tithes from the people, that is, from their kindred, though these also are descended from Abraham.
text.Rotherham: And, they, indeed, from among the sons of Levi who the priesthood receive, have commandment to take tithes of the people, according to the law, that is, of their brethren, although sprung from the loins of Abraham;
text.DRB: And certes they of the sons of Levi that take the priesthood have commandment to take tithes of the people according to the Law, that is to say, of their brethren: albeit themselves also issued out of the loins of Abraham.
text.KJV: And verily they that are of the sons of Levi, who receive the office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priesthood have a commandment according to the law to exact tithes from the people, that is, from their brothers, although they also have come from the loins of Abraham.

text.NRSV: But this man, who does not belong to their ancestry, collected tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had received the promises.
text.Rotherham: But, he who deriveth not his pedigree from among them, hath taken tithes of Abraham. And, the holder of the promises, hath he blessed.
text.DRB: But he whose generation is not numbered among them, took tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.
text.KJV: But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.
text.NABRE: But he who was not of their ancestry received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had received the promises.

text.NRSV: It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
text.Rotherham: But, apart from all gainsaying, the less, by the greater, is blessed.
text.DRB: But without all contradiction, that which is less, is blessed of the better.
text.KJV: And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.
text.NABRE: Unquestionably, a lesser person is blessed by a greater.

text.NRSV: In the one case, tithes are received by those who are mortal; in the other, by one of whom it is testified that he lives.
text.Rotherham: And, here, indeed, dying men take tithes, but, there, one of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.
text.DRB: And here indeed, men that die, receive tithes: but there he hath witness, that he liveth.
text.KJV: And here men that die receive tithes; but there he receiveth them, of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.
text.NABRE: In the one case, mortal men receive tithes; in the other, a man of whom it is testified that he lives on.

text.NRSV: One might even say that Levi himself, who receives tithes, paid tithes through Abraham,
text.Rotherham: And—so to say a word—through Abraham, even Levi who taketh tithes hath paid tithes;
text.DRB: And (that it may so be said) by Abraham Levi also, which received tithes, was tithed.
text.KJV: And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.
text.NABRE: One might even say that Levi himself, who receives tithes, was tithed through Abraham,

text.NRSV: for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
text.Rotherham: For, even then, was he, in the loins of his father, when, Melchizedek, met him.
text.DRB: For as yet he was in his father's loins, when Melchisedech met him.
text.KJV: For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.
text.NABRE: for he was still in his father’s loins when Melchizedek met him.

text.NRSV: Now if perfection had been attainable through the levitical priesthood—for the people received the law under this priesthood—what further need would there have been to speak of another priest arising according to the order of Melchizedek, rather than one according to the order of Aaron?
text.Rotherham: If indeed, therefore, there had been, a perfecting through means of the Levitical priesthood,—for, the people, thereon, have had based a code of laws, what further need, according to the rank of Melchizedek, for a different priest to be raised up, and, not according to the rank of Aaron, to be designated?
text.DRB: If then consummation was by the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people received the Law) what necessity was there yet another priest to rise according to the order of Melchisedech, and not to be called according to the order of Aaron?
text.KJV: If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need was there that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?
text.NABRE: If, then, perfection came through the levitical priesthood, on the basis of which the people received the law, what need would there still have been for another priest to arise according to the order of Melchizedek, and not reckoned according to the order of Aaron?

text.NRSV: For when there is a change in the priesthood, there is necessarily a change in the law as well.
text.Rotherham: For, seeing there is to be a change of the priesthood, of necessity, of law too, a change cometh.
text.DRB: For the priesthood being translated, it is necessary that a translation of the Law also be made.
text.KJV: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.
text.NABRE: When there is a change of priesthood, there is necessarily a change of law as well.

text.NRSV: Now the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.
text.Rotherham: For, he as to whom these things are said, with a different tribe, hath taken partnership, from which, no one, hath given attendance at the altar;
text.DRB: For he on whom these things be said, is of another tribe, of the which, none attended on the altar.
text.KJV: For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.
text.NABRE: Now he of whom these things are said belonged to a different tribe, of which no member ever officiated at the altar.

text.NRSV: For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah, and in connection with that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.
text.Rotherham: For it is, very evident, that out of Judah hath sprung our Lord,—respecting which tribe, concerning priests, nothing did Moses speak,
text.DRB: For it is manifest that our Lord sprung of Juda: in the which tribe Moyses spake nothing of priests.
text.KJV: For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.
text.NABRE: It is clear that our Lord arose from Judah, and in regard to that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.

text.NRSV: It is even more obvious when another priest arises, resembling Melchizedek,
text.Rotherham: And, yet more abundantly evident it is—that, according to the likeness of Melchizedek, there is to be raised up, a different priest.
text.DRB: And yet it is much more evident: if according to the similitude of Melchisedech there arise another priest,
text.KJV: And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,
text.NABRE: It is even more obvious if another priest is raised up after the likeness of Melchizedek,

text.NRSV: one who has become a priest, not through a legal requirement concerning physical descent, but through the power of an indestructible life.
text.Rotherham: Who, not according to a law of commandment dealing with the flesh, hath arisen, but according to the power of an indissoluble life;
text.DRB: which was not made according to the Law of the carnal commandment, but according to the power of life indissoluble.
text.KJV: Who is made, not after the law of a carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.
text.NABRE: who has become so, not by a law expressed in a commandment concerning physical descent but by the power of a life that cannot be destroyed.

text.NRSV: For it is attested of him, “You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.”
text.Rotherham: For it is witnessed—Thou, art a priest, age-abidingly, according to the rank of Melchizedek.
text.DRB: For he witnesseth, That thou art a priest for ever, according to the order of Melchisedech.
text.KJV: For he testifieth, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
text.NABRE: For it is testified: “You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.”

text.NRSV: There is, on the one hand, the abrogation of an earlier commandment because it was weak and ineffectual
text.Rotherham: For, a setting aside, doth, indeed, take place, of a foregoing commandment, by reason of its own weakness and unprofitableness,—
text.DRB: Reprobation certes is made of the former commandment, because of the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
text.KJV: For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
